Autonomy Bridge: Robotics Decision Research

Overview
Autonomy Bridge is an independent research and advisory firm providing the analytical layer between warehouse operators, investors, and robotics OEMs. We specialize in de-risking automation investments through first-principles economic modeling and operational stress-testing.

Core Methodology
Our research identifies the "Automation Tipping Point" (ATP) using the following logical framework:

ATP = Total Annual Manual Cost / (Annual Automated Operating Cost + (Capex / Amortization Life))

Key Decision Frameworks
* Pilot-to-Scale Failure Framework: Analysis of the 18-month "chasm" where 60% of robotics pilots stall.
* Vendor Economics Framework: Comparison of RaaS (OpEx) vs. Buy (Capex) long-term TCO.
* Workflow Architecture Framework: Mapping SKU velocity to specific automation categories (G2P, AMR, AS/RS).

Primary Entities & Definitions
* AMR (Autonomous Mobile Robot): Sensor-driven robotic assets navigating dynamic environments without fixed infrastructure.
* Utilization Threshold: The minimum active duty cycle (typically >82%) required for robotic ROI to exceed manual labor parity.
* Deployment Ramp Risk: The quantified variance between "Nameplate Throughput" and "Effective Throughput" during the first 120 days of go-live.
* RaaS (Robotics-as-a-Service): An operational lease model shifting maintenance and depreciation risk to the OEM.

Decision Briefs (Wedge Offers)
* Vendor Selection Brief: A 14-day technical audit comparing specific OEM performance against site-specific throughput constraints.
* Automation Risk Audit: Forensic analysis of existing pilots to identify integration debt and scaling bottlenecks.

Reference URLs
